title: Tamo Lan
content:

'''Tamo Lan''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Carosite]] field [[Medic/Legends|medic]] who served with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. After leaving his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], [[Carosi XII]], following an incident that involved him [[Death/Legends|killing]] a [[Unidentified Twi'lek (Carosi XII)|enslaver]] who threatened one of his [[Unidentified patient (Carosi XII)|patients]], Lan joined the Alliance and served on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Baes Logia]] and the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] before being selected to join [[Shroud Team]], an all-[[Alien/Legends|alien]] unit of [[Foster Agent]]s led by [[Jin]] [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] [[Dutra Zeneta]]. Foster Agents were responsible for returning stranded field agents to the Alliance, and Shroud Team operated out of [[Corint City]] on the planet [[Pirik]]. Lan served as the team's medic, while also training the [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] female [[Gondara]]—the sister of his deceased best friend, [[Karlon]]—to be his assistant.

==Biography==
Tamo Lan, a [[Carosite]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Carosi XII]]<ref name="Shroud" /> between [[28 BBY/Legends|28 BBY]] and [[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]].<ref name="TL-age" /> Like all Carosites, he had a natural affinity for the healing arts, and trained as a field [[Medic/Legends|medic]]. Lan also harbored the desire to leave his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] and join the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] in its [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|struggle]] against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. One [[Standard day/Legends|day]], Lan encountered a [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Unidentified Twi'lek (Carosi XII)|enslaver]] who threatened one of the Carosite's [[Unidentified patient (Carosi XII)|patients]]. The medic protected his ward, but to do so, he had to [[Death/Legends|kill]] the [[Slavery/Legends|enslaver]]. While Lan was not [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|banished]] from Carosi XII for his actions, he knew that it was in his own best interests if he left. The Carosite followed his desire and joined the Alliance sometime before [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], serving as a medic.<ref name="Shroud" />

Lan served throughout the Alliance, including the [[Baes Logia campaign|campaign]] on the planet [[Baes Logia]] and as part of the personnel staffing [[Great Temple/Legends|Headquarters Base]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] a little while prior to the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|destruction]] of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first]] [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]]. During his [[Time/Legends|time]] with the Alliance, Lan met an [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] male, [[Karlon]], and the two became best friends. Sometime after the Battle of [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin]], the [[Jin]] [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] [[Dutra Zeneta]] selected Lan to be part of [[Shroud Team]], a unit of all-[[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Foster Agent]]s based out of [[Corint City]] on the planet [[Pirik]] in the [[Divis Arm/Legends|Divis Arm]]. Shroud Team's composition of non-[[Human/Legends|Human]] [[operative]]s allowed them to blend into the population of Corint [[City/Legends|City]] effectively, as only five percent of the city's 10 million residents were Human.<ref name="Shroud" /> Operating as a Foster Agent was a dangerous role within the Alliance; they were responsible for returning stranded operatives—known as "orphans"—to the Alliance. Foster Agent names and locations were well-known to field operatives, which placed the Agents at risk if their potential charges were ever captured and interrogated.<ref name="GG9">''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]''</ref>

Lan, code-named "Shroud Four," was the team's medic and was often called upon to treat the injuries of both the members of Shroud Team and the various operatives who came into the unit's care. Sometime after [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], his friend Karlon was [[Battle of Bresnia|killed]] by Imperial forces on the planet [[Bresnia]]. Lan took charge of his friend's seventeen-[[Standard year/Legends|year]]-old sister, [[Gondara]]—who had been living on an Alliance [[Safe world/Legends|safe world]] while her brother fought for the Rebellion—and began training her as his assistant.<ref name="Shroud" />

==Personality and traits==
Tamo Lan possessed dark [[Color/Legends|brown]] fur and had small, dark [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. Like all Carosites, Lan held an optimistic outlook on life. However, his experiences with the Alliance during the Galactic Civil War dulled his optimism, and while he still had a positive disposition, it was tempered by observing the tyranny of the Empire and the horrors of war firsthand. A dedicated professional, Lan possessed the medical aptitude present in all Carosites. He was also extremely protective of his patients, the helpless and the young, and his ward, Gondara.<ref name="Shroud" />

==Skills and abilities==
A skilled and highly competent medic, Lan was trained in first-aid techniques and advanced medicine. He was knowledgeable about various alien [[Species/Legends|species]] and their cultures and [[Language/Legends|languages]], and was a personable individual, able to bargain or persuade others to achieve his goals. The Carosite's [[Technician/Legends|technical]] skills allowed him to operate communications and [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] equipment, interface with security systems, and repair and program [[Computer/Legends|computers]]. Lan could also program [[Droid/Legends|droids]], with a specialization in working with [[2-1B surgical droid/Legends|2-1B surgical droids]]. He was capable of [[Astrogation/Legends|plotting]] courses through [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] and was trained to drive and repair [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] vehicles. Despite being a medic, Lan was trained to use a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]], was competent at [[Melee weapon/Legends|melee]] and [[Unarmed combat/Legends|unarmed combat]], and was trained in urban survival techniques.<ref name="Shroud" />

==Equipment==
Tamo Lan generally armed himself with a [[Heavy blaster pistol/Legends|heavy blaster pistol]], along with a [[Knife/Legends|knife]]. In addition to his weapons, he carried a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]], a [[Medical diagnostic computer|diagnostic computer]], and three [[Medpac/Legends|medpacs]].<ref name="Shroud" />

==Behind the scenes==
Tamo Lan was featured in "[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives (article)|Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]," a series of articles published in issues of [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]''. Lan was featured in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 10|issue 10's]] article, released in [[May]] [[1996]]. It was written by [[Craig Robert Carey|C. Robert Carey]] and featured a black-and-white illustration of the Carosite by [[Kathy Burdette]].<ref name="Shroud" />
